metrics.reason: Primary indicator – shows whether the Neo4j service is running or stopped.
Remote Procedure Call service; many Windows services depend on it. If RPC is down, Neo4j may fail to start.
Server service – provides SMB shares used by Neo4j for data files. Its status can affect service start‑up.
Workstation service – required for network file access. Issues here can prevent Neo4j from accessing its data directory.
Azure Guest Agent interacts with the underlying VM platform; failures may indicate broader VM issues that affect Neo4j.
Zabbix agent itself; if it is not running, metric collection may be incomplete, affecting RCA reliability.
Overall CPU utilization – high CPU can prevent services from starting or cause timeouts.
Shows time spent in kernel mode; spikes may indicate service start‑up contention.
User‑mode CPU usage; helps correlate CPU pressure with service failure.
Length of the ready queue; a high value indicates CPU scheduling bottlenecks that can delay service start.
Rate of page reads/writes; elevated values suggest memory pressure that could affect Neo4j.
Number of page faults per second; high faults indicate insufficient RAM for Neo4j.
Paging file usage; excessive paging can degrade performance and prevent service start.
Overall memory utilization percentage; helps confirm if the host is out of RAM.
Absolute amount of RAM used; useful together with total size for capacity analysis.
Total RAM installed; required to calculate utilization and assess if memory is a limiting factor.
Percentage of free swap space; low free swap can indicate memory exhaustion.
Absolute free swap space; complements the percentage metric.
Percentage of used space on C: drive where Neo4j binaries/logs often reside.
Percentage of used space on D: drive where Neo4j data directory is commonly placed.
Free space on C: – ensures there is room for logs and temporary files.
Free space on D: – critical for Neo4j data writes.
Inbound network errors on the primary NIC; network issues can prevent Neo4j from binding to its port.
Outbound network errors; may affect cluster communication or client connections.
Operational status of the NIC (up/down). A down interface would explain service unavailability.
Host uptime; a recent reboot could explain why Neo4j has not yet started.
Identifies the host in multi‑host environments; useful for correlating logs.
Confirms Zabbix agent connectivity; if false, metric collection may be incomplete.
